12 March 2026 Military News

News Reports

Current Operations

    Other Operations

    Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • Iran's Navy Is Largely Gone. The Threat To The Strait Of Hormuz Is Not. RFE/RL 12 Mar 2026 -- The United States and Israel have largely destroyed Iran's conventional naval fleet in a massive bombing campaign since February 28.
  • US-Israeli Bombing Of Iran's Energy Infrastructure Marks New Escalation In War RFE/RL 12 Mar 2026 -- The United States and Israel are bombing Iran's critical energy and civilian infrastructure, marking an escalation in the military campaign against the Islamic republic, experts say.
  • Georgian Sailors Survive Iranian Attack On Ship Off Iraqi Coast RFE/RL 12 Mar 2026 -- All 23 Georgian crew members aboard a Malta-flagged tanker struck by Iran off the Iraqi coast have been rescued, a relative of one of the sailors told RFE/RL's Georgian Service.

  • Loss of U.S. KC-135 Over Iraq CENTCOM 12 Mar 2026 -- U.S. Central Command is aware of the loss of a U.S. KC-135 refueling aircraft. The incident occurred in friendly airspace during Operation Epic Fury, and rescue efforts are ongoing. Two aircraft were involved in the incident. One of the aircraft went down in western Iraq, and the second landed safely.
  • Pentagon estimates $11.3 billion spent in first six days of war on Iran Press TV 12 Mar 2026 -- The administration of US President Donald Trump has estimated that the cost of the first six days of its ongoing war on Iran topped $11 billion, as the unprovoked war of aggression enters its 13th day.
  • First Week of Operation Against Iran Cost US Over $11.3B - Reports Sputnik 12 Mar 2026 -- The first week of the US operation against Iran cost more than $11.3 billion, The New York Times reported, citing three officials.
  • Pentagon says first 6 days of U.S. strikes on Iran cost 11.3 bln USD People's Daily 12 Mar 2026 -- Pentagon officials have told U.S. lawmakers that the first six days of the U.S. military campaign against Iran cost more than 11.3 billion U.S. dollars, The New York Times reported on Wednesday.
  • U.S. missile behind strike on Iranian elementary school that killed over 160: preliminary probe People's Daily 12 Mar 2026 -- An ongoing military investigation has preliminarily determined that a U.S. Tomahawk missile strike on an Iranian elementary school that killed over 160 people, mostly pupils, resulted from a targeting error, The New York Times reported Wednesday.

  • IRGC: Resistance groups strike down US Air Force KC-135 aircraft in western Iraq Press TV 12 Mar 2026 -- Iraq's resistance groups have struck down a US Air Force KC-135 Stratotanker, a symbol of American overreach and aggression.
  • Iran's Army vows to 'stand until last breath' after new Leader's command Press TV 12 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Army has vowed to continue fighting to the last breath and avenge the blood of martyrs, hours after the new Leader of the Islamic Revolution, Ayatollah Seyyed Mojtaba Khamenei, called for a strong and effective response to the US-Israeli aggression.
  • IRGC hits US Fifth Fleet base in Bahrain with missiles, drones Press TV 12 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) says it has launched a devastating missile and drone strike on the US Navy's Fifth Fleet base in Mina Salman, Bahrain, shortly after the first message from the new leader of the Islamic Revolution, Ayatollah Seyyed Mojtaba Khamenei, emphasized Iran's "determined and regret-inducing" defense efforts.
  • True Promise 4: Iran and resistance axis ops. against US-Israeli assets on Mar. 12 Press TV 12 Mar 2026 -- Iranian armed forces and resistance groups across the region continue to carry out retaliatory military operations against the United States and the Israeli regime.
  • IRGC launches new wave of heavy missiles on US-Israeli targets Press TV 12 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has announced the commencement of Wave 42 of Operation True Promise 4 following the US-Israeli aggression against Iran.
  • Iran launches wave 41 of Operation True Promise 4 with hypersonic, heavy missiles Press TV 12 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps has announced the launch of the 41st wave of Operation True Promise 4 against targets in Israeli-occupied territories in response to the ongoing US-Israeli aggression on Iran.
  • Iran Army launches drone operation, says radar collapse makes Israeli targets easier to hit Press TV 12 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Army says its operations against Israeli military infrastructure are becoming easier and more precise after parts of the regime's radar network were destroyed, as a new wave of drone strikes hit key bases and security facilities.

  • Congress To Be Briefed As Probe Examines Iran School Strike, Sources Tell RFE/RL RFE/RL 12 Mar 2026 -- A final assessment by US military officials of events surrounding a deadly missile strike on an Iranian girls' school will be shared with Congress in the coming days, sources told RFE/RL amid growing reports that the attack was likely launched by the United States.
  • Trump Says 'We Won' But Fight Will Go On, As Iran Targets Gulf Shipping RFE/RL 12 Mar 2026 -- US ⁠President Donald Trump claimed the United States has "won" the war against Iran but says its forces will continue to attack until the job is finished, while Tehran has hit at least six vessels in the region and vowed further strikes.
  • Trump sends mixed messages on when strikes on Iran will end People's Daily 12 Mar 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump said Wednesday the U.S. military operation in Iran is both a war and a short-term "excursion," sending mixed messages on when the strikes will end.
  • FBI warns Iran may attack California with drones: report People's Daily 12 Mar 2026 -- The U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) warned police departments in California in recent days that Iran could retaliate for American attacks by launching drones at the West Coast, ABC News reported on Wednesday, citing an alert reviewed by the news outlet.

  • In first address, Iran's new Leader stresses defense, need to keep Hormuz closed Press TV 12 Mar 2026 -- New Leader of the Islamic Revolution Ayatollah Seyyed Mojtaba Khamenei has issued his first message, stressing unity and the Iranian people's "determined and regret-inducing" defense efforts and asserting that the Strait of Hormuz must remain closed.
  • Iran To Keep Strait Of Hormuz Closed, Supreme Leader Khamenei Says In First Public Comments RFE/RL 12 Mar 2026 -- Iran must continue to keep a key Gulf shipping lane closed, ⁠Supreme ⁠Leader Mojtaba Khamenei said in his first public comments since being named to succeed his late father, hours after several vessels were targeted in strikes as Tehran looks to choke off oil supplies from leaving the Middle East.
  • Iran's Supreme Leader: 'All US Bases Should Be Closed, We Will Continue to Attack Them' Sputnik 12 Mar 2026 -- Former Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei was killed on February 28. Iran declared a 40-day period of mourning.
  • Araghchi criticizes US for its failure to engage constructively on nuclear matters Press TV 12 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi has reaffirmed Tehran's steadfast commitment to regional peace and stability, highlighting two critical issues that underscore the Islamic Republic's principled stance on global affairs.
  • Larijani warns Trump over threat to target Iran's electricity infrastructure Press TV 12 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Secretary of the Supreme National Security Council Ali Larijani has condemned US President Donald Trump for threatening to destroy Iran's electricity infrastructure, vowing that such an action would lead to severe consequences.
  • Iran says the shadow of war must be permanently lifted Press TV 12 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Foreign Ministry has emphasized the necessity of ensuring that the threat of war is permanently removed from the country, while also warning against allowing enemies to initiate aggression at will and then seek cessation only when faced with high costs.
  • US-Israeli war endangers food shipping routes for 300 million people: Iran to FAO Press TV 12 Mar 2026 -- In a warning notice to the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO), Iran says the US-Israeli war of aggression against the country has put the food shipping routes in jeopardy not only for Iran but for 300 million people living in West Asia and Eurasia.
  • Iran warns to shed invaders' blood in Persian Gulf if islands attacked Press TV 12 Mar 2026 -- Parliament Speaker Mohammad-Baqer Qalibaf has warned that Iran will make the Persian Gulf run with the blood of invaders if the country's islands are attacked.
  • Iran says enemy's claim of talks and ceasefire pointless while aggression continues Press TV 12 Mar 2026 -- The spokesman for Iran's Foreign Ministry says the country does not accept the enemy's claims of negotiations and ceasefire while they press ahead with their military aggression against the Islamic Republic, which has entered its 13th day.
  • Iran Does Not Consider Israel Party to Talks to Need Mediators With It - Ambassador Sputnik 12 Mar 2026 -- Iran does not need mediators with Israel because it does not consider it a party to negotiations, Iranian Ambassador to Moscow Kazem Jalali told Sputnik in an interview.
  • 'Unjust, unlawful': Iran doesn't recognize UNSC resolution on US-Israeli aggression Press TV 12 Mar 2026 -- The Iranian ambassador and permanent representative to the United Nations has expressed regret over the Security Council's adoption of a resolution against the Islamic Republic regarding the US-Israeli aggression, saying the country does not recognize the "unjust and unlawful" document.
  • Anti-Iran strikes from US military bases in region 'unacceptable': President Pezeshkian Press TV 12 Mar 2026 -- Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian warns that any attack launched from US military bases stationed in the region against the Islamic Republic are not be acceptable under any circumstances.

  • PM Netanyahu's Remarks at his Press Conference Israel - Prime Minister 12 Mar 2026 -- "We are in historic days, days that will be recorded in the annals of Israel. In Operation Roaring Lion, our roar is growing stronger. We are crushing the terrorist regime in Iran. We are striking and crushing its proxy Hezbollah in Lebanon."

  • With Middle East 'pushed to breaking point', UN chief urges ceasefire, return to diplomacy UN News 12 Mar 2026 -- The UN chief on Thursday urged all combatants to cease hostilities in the Middle East, uphold international law, protect civilians and make an immediate return to the negotiating table.
  • Aid operations strained across Middle East: WFP seeks $200 million to sustain food assistance UN News 12 Mar 2026 -- Rising food prices, growing displacement and disruptions to global supply routes are placing millions of people across the Middle East at greater risk of hunger, according to the World Food Programme (WFP), which warns that the humanitarian system is struggling to keep pace with expanding needs.
  • UNHCR: Up to 3.2 million Iranians temporarily displaced in Iran as conflict intensifies UNHCR 12 Mar 2026 -- Between 600,000 and 1 million Iranian households are now temporarily displaced inside Iran as a result of the ongoing conflict, according to preliminary assessments, representing up to 3.2 million people. Most of them are reportedly fleeing from Tehran and other major urban areas towards the north of the country and rural areas to seek safety. This figure is likely to continue rising as hostilities persist, marking a worrying escalation in humanitarian needs.
  • Security Council Debates Iran Nuclear Programme amid Dispute over 'Snapback' Sanctions as Russian Federation, China Challenge Legality UN Security Council 12 Mar 2026 -- The Security Council met today to debate Iran's nuclear programme amid rising tensions in the Middle East, as the Russian Federation and China challenged the legality of United Nations sanctions on Tehran under the "snapback" mechanism.
  • UN Security Council fails to pass resolution calling for immediate halt of military activities in Middle East People's Daily 12 Mar 2026 -- The UN Security Council on Wednesday failed to pass a draft resolution urging all parties to immediately stop their military activities and refrain from further escalation in the Middle East and beyond and condemning all attacks against civilians and civilian infrastructure.

  • Middle East tensions threaten biggest oil supply disruption in history, IEA says Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 12 Mar 2026 -- Escalating conflict in the Middle East threatens to cause the largest supply disruption in the history of the oil market, the International Energy Agency (IEA) said Thursday adding that about 7.9 million bpd of crude and 9.9 million bpd of total liquids could be shut in across Gulf producers in March, according to Anadolu Agency.

  • G7 Leaders' Online Meeting Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 12 Mar 2026

  • China regrets draft resolution on Mideast not adopted at UNSC People's Daily 12 Mar 2026 -- China is disappointed and regrets that the UN Security Council draft resolution on the situation in the Middle East was not adopted, said Fu Cong, China's permanent representative to the United Nations, on Wednesday.
  • China disappointed, regretful that draft resolution on Middle East ceasefire was not adopted: Chinese envoy to UN Global Times 12 Mar 2026 -- Following the UN Security Council's Wednesday vote on a draft resolution concerning the situation in the Middle East, Fu Cong, China's permanent representative to the United Nations, said in explanatory remarks that the draft resolution proposed by Russia reaffirms the purposes and principles of the UN Charter, urges all parties to immediately cease military actions, condemns all attacks against civilians and civilian infrastructure, and encourages all parties to return to the track of diplomatic negotiations. China welcomes and supports this, and was disappointed and regretful that the draft resolution was not adopted.
  • Strait of Hormuz tensions simmer as Iran war lasts; China steps up shuttle diplomacy Global Times 12 Mar 2026 -- The US-Israeli military strikes against Iran have entered 12th day with mounting civilian casualties, global oil prices lingering at high level and deepening risks of a full blown regional conflicts.
  • Chinese envoy urges U.S., Israel to cease military operations to prevent further deterioration of situation People's Daily 12 Mar 2026 -- The fundamental way to prevent further deterioration of the situation is for the United States and Israel to cease their military operations, a Chinese envoy said in his explanation of the vote on the UN Security Council draft resolution on the Iran-related situation on Wednesday.
  • Wang Yi Has a Phone Call with Qatari Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman Al Thani PRC MofA 12 Mar 2026
  • Wang Yi Has a Phone Call with Egyptian Foreign Minister Badr Abdelatty PRC MofA 12 Mar 2026

  • Russia to send medical aid to Iran through Azerbaijan Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 12 Mar 2026 -- The Ministry of the Russian Federation for Civil Defence, Emergencies and Elimination of Consequences of Natural Disasters (EMERCOM) will dispatch medical supplies to Azerbaijan for onward delivery to Iran on a special flight, the ministry's press service reports.
  • Russia, Iran Remain Interested in North-South Corridor Project - Kremlin Sputnik 12 Mar 2026 -- Russia, Iran and a group of regional countries remain interested in the International North-South Transport Corridor project, Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said on Thursday.
  • Russia, China lambast adoption of unilateral resolution against Iran at UNSC amid US-Israeli aggression Press TV 12 Mar 2026 -- Russia and China have lambasted the adoption of a unilateral resolution against Iran at the United Nations Security Council, as an unprovoked war of aggression by a US-Israeli military coalition on the Islamic Republic enters its 13th day.

  • Australia deploys Wedgetail to Gulf Australia DOD 12 Mar 2026 -- At the request of the Gulf nations, Australia has deployed an E-7A Wedgetail to the Gulf to help protect and defend Australians and other civilians.

  • Statement by the Spokesperson on UN Security Council Resolution 2817 European External Action Service (EEAS) 12 Mar 2026
  • Albares conveys Spain's support and solidarity to counterparts in Middle East Spain MFA 12 Mar 2026 -- The minister has spoken with the Foreign ministers of Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Lebanon, Oman and Bahrain
  • Ankara completely opposed to any plan to provoke civil war in Iran: Turkish foreign minister Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 12 Mar 2026 -- Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan said on Thursday that Ankara remains "completely opposed to any plan that aims to provoke civil war in Iran and fuel conflicts along ethnic or religious fault lines", according to Anadolu Agency.
  • "We are pursuing intensive diplomacy to prevent the escalation of the Iran-centered spiral of violence" Presidency of The Republic of Turkey 12 Mar 2026 -- Speaking at the Presentation Ceremony of Atatürk International Peace Prize to United Nations Secretary-General António Guterres, President Erdoğan said: "As Türkiye, we are pursuing intensive diplomacy to prevent the escalation of the Iran-centered spiral of violence that carries the risk of dragging our geography into an all-out catastrophe. Despite those seeking to dishearten and deter us from the fight, we will continue to work determinedly and patiently."
  • Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi speaks with the President of Iran India PIB 12 Mar 2026 -- Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi had a telephone conversation today with the President of the Islamic Republic of Iran, H
  • Aboul Gheit condemns targeting of Oman's Port of Salalah League of Arab States 12 Mar 2026 -- Mr. Ahmed Aboul Gheit reiterated his condemnation, in the strongest terms, of the flagrant Iranian attack that targeted the strategic Port of Salalah in the Sultanate of Oman using drones, resulting in damage to a number of fuel storage tanks at the port.
  • The telephone calls held by HM King Mohammed VI, may God assist Him, with several GCC leaders following the abject Iranian aggression, are the highest expression of Morocco's unwavering and constant brotherly solidarity with these States, MFA Nasser Bourita stated on Thursday Morocco Ministry of Foreign Affairs 12 Mar 2026
  • US-Israeli war of aggression on Iran meant to reshape region: Omani FM Press TV 12 Mar 2026 -- Oman's Foreign Minister Sayyid Badr Albusaidi says that the United States and Israel launched an all-out coordinated aggression against Iran to block the Palestinian statehood and reshape the West Asia region.
  • Italy Won't Join War Against Iran, Meloni Affirms Sputnik 12 Mar 2026 -- Italian Prime Minister Georgia Meloni has voiced opposition to the ongoing US-Israeli hostilities against Iran.
  • The UK remains committed to finding a lasting and sustainable solution that ensures Iran never acquires a nuclear weapon: UK statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 12 Mar 2026 -- Statement by Ambassador James Kariuki, UK Deputy Permanent Representative to the UN, at the UN Security Council meeting on Iran.
  • Foreign Secretary touches down in the Gulf to demonstrate support for regional allies facing Iranian aggression UK FCDO 12 Mar 2026 -- Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper travels to Saudi Arabia to show UK support for regional allies as they defend themselves from reckless Iranian aggression
  • HE GCCSG: The UN Security Council Resolution Condemning Treacherous Iranian Attacks on GCC States and Jordan with Unprecedented International Consensus is Blatant Evidence of Iran's Violation of International Laws, Norms, and Charters Gulf Cooperation Council 12 Mar 2026

  • Ariel Cohen: Iran's Energy Pressure Campaign 'Ultimately Unsustainable' RFE/RL 12 Mar 2026 -- With tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz down sharply since the start of the US-Israeli war with Iran and oil prices up amid fears of Iranian attacks on Gulf energy infrastructure, analysts increasingly warn that global energy flows themselves are becoming part of the strategic battlefield.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Eucom Commander Says Military Relationship With NATO Partners Remains Strong DoD News 12 Mar 2026 -- During a Senate Armed Services Committee hearing on Capitol Hill today, the commander of U.S. European Command and supreme allied commander Europe said the U.S. military's relationship with its NATO partners throughout the region remains strong.
  • Naval Facilities Command Drives Mission Success in Caribbean DoD News 12 Mar 2026 -- Naval Facilities Engineering Systems Command Southeast rapidly delivered critical engineering, real estate, environmental and contracting support that directly enabled U.S. military operations across the Caribbean, accelerating infrastructure readiness in support of Operation Southern Spear.
  • Kentucky National Guard 'Ready for the Future,' Army Guard Director tells leaders Army News 12 Mar 2026 -- The Director of the Army National Guard praised the Kentucky National Guard's combat readiness, domestic response capabilities and international partnerships during remarks at the National Guard Association of Kentucky conference here, Feb. 28.
  • JIATF-401 Leads Groundbreaking U.S.-U.K. Agreement on Counter-Drone Technology Standards Army News 12 Mar 2026 -- In a significant step to enhance allied military interoperability, the U.S. Department of War and the United Kingdom's Ministry of Defence have signed a landmark Joint Declaration of Intent to establish common data standards for counter-unmanned aerial system technologies. This crucial effort was led by Joint Interagency Task Force 401, the organization at the forefront of fielding counter-drone capabilities.
  • Navy Accepts Delivery of Ship to Shore Connector, LCAC 115 US Navy 12 Mar 2026 -- The U.S. Navy accepted delivery of Ship to Shore Connector (SSC), Landing Craft Air Cushion (LCAC) 115 from Textron Systems on March 10.

  • First Stryker vehicles delivered by NSPA's Operations and Support Office NATO Support and Procurement Agency (NSPA) 12 Mar 2026 -- The NATO Support and Procurement Agency (NSPA), through its Operations and Support Office, has successfully completed the first shipment of a number of Stryker armoured vehicles to the Bulgarian Armed Forces, with delivery on 20 February 2026.
  • NATO Secretary General highlights crucial role of industry in our security NATO 12 Mar 2026 -- On Thursday (12 March 2026), NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte attended the Brussels European Defense Exhibition (BEDEX). He praised the crucial role of the defence industry for continued security across Europe and North America.

  • Volodymyr Zelenskyy and Nicușor Dan Signed Documents Between Ukraine and Romania on Establishing a Strategic Partnership, Cooperation in the Energy Sector, and Joint Weapons Production President of Ukraine 12 Mar 2026
  • The President of Ukraine and the President of Romania Agreed to Deepen Economic, Energy, and Defense Cooperation President of Ukraine 12 Mar 2026
  • Ukraine is the first country in the world to open real battlefield data to partners for AI model training Ukraine MOD 12 Mar 2026

  • Lawmakers give Cabinet nod to sign LOAs for 4 U.S. weapons systems CNA 12 Mar 2026 -- The Legislature is expected to allow the Cabinet to sign Letters of Offer and Acceptance (LOA) for four U.S. weapons systems due to expire this month, following cross-party talks Thursday.

  • Exercise Milan brings action and milestones Australia DOD 12 Mar 2026 -- HMAS Warramunga has completed the sea phase of Exercise Milan in the Bay of Bengal, ending five days of high‑end maritime training alongside international partners.

  • Israel MOD Director General Tours Rafael's Aerial Defense Production Lines Amid Wartime Accelerated Manufacturing Effort Israel MOD 12 Mar 2026 -- Israel Ministry of Defense (IMOD) Director General, Maj. Gen. (Res.) Amir Baram, visited Rafael's production lines and development laboratories, where he held a situational assessment with Company Chairman Prof. Yuval Steinitz, CEO Yoav Tourgeman, and other senior defense officials.

  • Scotland set for skills and innovation boost as £50 million defence growth deal unveiled UK MOD 12 Mar 2026 -- Scotland's crucial role in UK defence is to be boosted, as Minister for Defence Readiness and Industry, Luke Pollard and Secretary of State for Scotland, Douglas Alexander today launched a landmark £50 million Scotland Defence Growth Deal.

  • Vice Minister of National Defence T. Godliauskas discussed strengthening of the Eastern border in Poland Lithuania MND 12 Mar 2026 -- On March 10 Vice Minister of National Defence Tomas Godliauskas paid a visit to Warsaw. He met with the Polish counterpart Cezary Tomczyk and other MoD officials responsible for public preparedness for defence and formation of the active reserve.

  • Poland and Sweden will develop military capabilities together to strengthen security on the Baltic Sea Poland - Ministry of National Defence 12 Mar 2026 -- On Thursday, President Gitanas Nausėda met with the President of the Czech Republic, Petr Pavel, who arrived in Lithuania on a state visit.

  • Defence Minister's meeting with the representatives of GDELS-RO-UMB's Association Coordination Board Romania - Ministry of National Defence 12 Mar 2026 -- On Tuesday, March 10, Minister of National Defence, Radu Miruță, had a meeting, at the MoND headquarters, with representatives of the Association Coordination Board between General Dynamics European Land Systems Romania and Bucharest Mechanical Plant.

  • Bulgaria to host joint Bulgarian-Greek Air Force training flights Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 12 Mar 2026 -- Joint training flights of the Bulgarian and Greek Air Forces will take place in Bulgarian airspace, the Bulgarian News Agency (BTA) reported citing the Bulgarian Defence Ministry.

  • Secretary Rubio's Call with German Foreign Minister Wadephul US Dept. of State 12 Mar 2026

    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 12 March 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 12 Mar 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • Statement by PM Netanyahu Israel - Prime Minister 12 Mar 2026 -- "The blood libel against the fighters of Force 100, known as the 'Sde Teiman affair', which defamed Israel worldwide on an unprecedented scale, has come to an end."

  • Hezbollah Strikes Back: Israel's Iron Dome Overwhelmed Sputnik 12 Mar 2026 -- In early March, the Israeli army announced the launch of strikes against Hezbollah targets in Lebanon. In response, Hezbollah retaliated, escalating tensions between the two sides.

  • UNRWA Situation Report #212 on the humanitarian crisis in the Gaza Strip and the occupied West Bank, including East Jerusalem UNRWA 12 Mar 2026

  • IOM Chief Condemns Beirut Strike Killing Displaced Civilians IOM 12 Mar 2026 -- The Director General of the International Organization for Migration (IOM), Amy Pope, is gravely alarmed by reports of a strike that hit displaced people in Beirut, killing eight people and leaving dozens injured.
  • UN experts denounce aggression on Iran and Lebanon, warn of devastating regional escalation OHCHR 12 Mar 2026 -- UN experts* today condemned the ongoing military assaults on Iran and Lebanon by the United States and Israel as flagrant violations of international law.
  • 'Perfect storm': Lebanon crisis deepens as civilians bear the brunt UN News 12 Mar 2026 -- Lebanon is facing a "perfect storm of unpredictable challenges" as conflict, mass displacement and dwindling humanitarian resources converge, the UN's Resident and Humanitarian Coordinator in Lebanon, Imran Riza, has warned.

  • United States Sanctions Hamas's Illicit Charity Network US Dept. of State 12 Mar 2026 -- "Today, the United States is sanctioning four illicit charities that directly fund Hamas militants."
  • Treasury Continues to Disrupt Hamas' Sham Charity Network as the Group Refuses to Disarm US Treasury 12 Mar 2026 -- Today, the Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) is designating four sham charities that directly fund Hamas's Military Wing and its terrorist activities militants and enable the group to sustain its terrorist operations.

  • Canada providing aid to help address growing humanitarian needs in Lebanon Canada 12 Mar 2026 -- The Lebanese people are bearing the brunt of the ongoing conflict between Israel and Hezbollah. Mass displacement, disrupted markets and rising food insecurity have led to an urgent need for shelter, food, clean water and health care products in Lebanon.
  • Lebanon - 60 tonnes of humanitarian cargo delivered to Lebanon (12 March 2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 12 Mar 2026 -- The situation of hundreds of thousands of displaced people in Lebanon is an issue of grave concern. Given the emergency, the Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs (MEAE) is playing an active role. In partnership with the company CMA CGM, the MEAE's Crisis and Support Centre (CDCS) shipped nearly 60 tonnes of humanitarian aid on Thursday 12 March.
  • Iceland intervenes in South Africa case at the International Court of Justice Government of Iceland 12 Mar 2026 -- The Government of Iceland has filed a declaration of intervention in the case lodged by South Africa against Israel before the International Court of Justice.
  • General Secretariat statement on closure of Al-Aqsa Mosque League of Arab States 12 Mar 2026 -- The General Secretariat of the League of Arab States, represented by the Palestine and Occupied Arab Territories Sector, expresses its profound concern over the continued measures taken by the Israeli occupation authorities to close Al-Aqsa Mosque and Al-Haram Al-Sharif to Muslim worshippers, particularly during the holy month of Ramadan.
  • Spanish Government expresses utmost concern at serious deterioration of situation in Lebanon Spain MFA 12 Mar 2026 -- Spain recognises the efforts of the Lebanese Government to achieve a State monopoly on the use of force and condemns Hezbollah's indiscriminate attacks against Israel, which could lead to a new spiral of violence and suffering.
  • No: 43, 12 March 2026, Regarding Israel's Attacks on Lebanon Republic of Turkey Ministry of Foreign Affairs 12 Mar 2026

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Middle East, Lebanon, Occupied Palestinian Territory, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Sudan UNOCHA 12 Mar 2026

  • PCG: WPS as PH territory based on int'l laws, not idle talk Philippine News Agency 12 Mar 2026 -- The Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) said the West Philippine Sea (WPS) is an undisputed part of Philippine territory based on international laws and the 2016 Arbitral Award, not mere "satsat" or idle talk.

  • The Cooperation Council for the Arab States of the Gulf (GCC) has reiterated its consistent position of support for Morocco's sovereignty over the Sahara, and its commitment to the Kingdom's security, stability and territorial integrity. Morocco Ministry of Foreign Affairs 12 Mar 2026

  • Ranking Member Shaheen, Representative Turner Lead Bicameral, Bipartisan Letter Pressing State Department on Potential Drawdown of U.S. Forces from NATO Kosovo Mission US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 12 Mar 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senators Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, Chuck Grassley (R-IA), Thom Tillis (R-NC), Roger Wicker (R-MS), Chris Van Hollen (D-MD), Joni Ernst (R-IA) and Peter Welch (D-VT) and Representatives Mike Turner (R-OH), Brendan Boyle (D-PA), Gregory W. Meeks (D-NY), Ranking Member of the House Foreign Affairs Committee, Rick Larsen (D-WA) and Ann Wagner (R-MO) sent a letter to Secretary of State Marco Rubio raising serious concerns about reports that the Trump Administration may draw down U.S. forces from the NATO-led Kosovo Force (KFOR) mission in Kosovo.

  • Sudan: Türk appalled by surge in deadly drone attacks on civilians in Kordofan and White Nile OHCHR 12 Mar 2026 -- UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Türk said on Thursday he was appalled by the devastating impact on civilians of increasing drone attacks, amid reports that more than 200 civilians have been killed by drones since 4 March alone in the Kordofan region and in White Nile state.

  • Commemorating the victims of the Armed Conflict on the Nistru River: UK statement to the OSCE UK FCDO 12 Mar 2026 -- On the 34th anniversary of the outbreak of the conflict on the Nistru River, UK Chargé d'Affaires, Deputy Ambassador James Ford, honours the victims and all those affected.

News Reports

  • Prime Minister's Office releases new overview of Russian information influence activities targeting Finland Finnish Government 12 Mar 2026 -- In 2025, Russia's discourse about Finland took on a more hostile tone. Russia's increasingly aggressive messaging is a reaction to Finland's stronger cooperation within NATO, among other things. Russia also tends to harden its rhetoric towards its opponents as its own situation becomes more difficult.

  • Intelligence authorities to gain more extensive rights to disclose criminal information Finnish Government 12 Mar 2026 -- The Finnish Security and Intelligence Service and military intelligence authorities will be able to disclose information on offences to other authorities more extensively than before.

  • Another intervention against a false flagged vessel Swedish Coast Guard 12 Mar 2026 -- Around 8:30 pm on Thursday evening, the Swedish Coast Guard boarded a 228-metre tanker off Trelleborg in the Baltic Sea. A preliminary investigation into suspected violations regarding lack of seaworthiness has been initiated. The operation is carried out with the support of Swedish Police.

  • 69th session of the Commission on Narcotic Drugs, Vienna, 9-13 March 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 12 Mar 2026

  • Russia Sentences 15 To Life In Prison Over Deadly 2024 Moscow Terror Attack RFE/RL 12 Mar 2026 -- A Russian military court has sentenced four men, all Tajik nationals, and their 11 alleged accomplices to life imprisonment for carrying out the terror attack on the Crocus City Hall entertainment center near Moscow in March 2024, which killed 149 people.
  • Fifteen Defendants in Crocus Terrorist Attack Case Receive Life Sentences Sputnik 12 Mar 2026 -- The 2nd Western District Military Court sentenced 15 out of 19 defendants in the Crocus City Hall terrorist attack near Moscow to life imprisonment, a Sputnik correspondent reported from the courtroom.
  • Meeting with Governor of Orenburg Region Yevgeny Solntsev President of Russia 12 Mar 2026 -- The President held a working meeting with Governor of Orenburg Region Yevgeny Solntsev.
  • Armenian Refusal of Russian Aid Suspicious - Russian Foreign Ministry Sputnik 12 Mar 2026 -- Russian Foreign Ministry spokeswoman Maria Zakharova has criticized Armenia's rejection of humanitarian aid for refugees from the Nagorno-Karabakh region.
  • Turkey, Central Asia Look To Slovakia To Get Russian Oligarch Usmanov Off EU Sanctions List RFE/RL 12 Mar 2026 -- Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan, Azerbaijan, and three Central Asian republics are, via Slovakia, pushing to get Russian-Uzbek oligarch Alisher Usmanov removed from the European Union's sanctions list ahead of a 15 March rollover of the bloc's extensive blacklist.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun's Regular Press Conference on March 12, 2026 PRC MofA 12 Mar 2026
  • China's top political advisory body concludes annual session Global Times 12 Mar 2026 -- The 14th National Committee of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference (CPPCC), China's top political advisory body, concluded its annual session on Wednesday morning, Xinhua News Agency reported.
  • China's new five-year plan charts world's largest modernization by population People's Daily 12 Mar 2026 -- With a new development blueprint under review at the ongoing national legislative session, China is embracing a critical five-year period in an ambitious drive to basically modernize its vast population by 2035, on a scale unseen in human history.
  • Wang Yi Has a Phone Call with Pakistani Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Mohammad Ishaq Dar PRC MofA 12 Mar 2026 -- On March 10, 2026, Member of the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee and Foreign Minister Wang Yi had a phone call with Pakistani Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Mohammad Ishaq Dar at the latter's request.
  • Wang Yi Has a Phone Call with Member of the Political Bureau of the Central Committee of the Communist Party of Cuba and Cuban Foreign Minister Bruno Rodriguez Parrilla PRC MofA 12 Mar 2026
  • Chinese commercial space firm targets SQX-3 maiden launch in 2026 Global Times 12 Mar 2026 -- China's commercial aerospace company iSpace announced on Thursday that its self-developed SQX-3 carrier rocket is scheduled to conduct its maiden launch in 2026, the Global Times learned from the company.

  • Nearly 60% would 'resist' Chinese invasion without U.S. help: Survey CNA 12 Mar 2026 -- Nearly 60 percent of respondents in Taiwan said they would "resist" a Chinese invasion even without U.S. military support, while fewer now view the United States as a credible country, according to results of an "American Portrait Project" survey released Thursday by Academia Sinica.
  • Chinese warplane lull around Taiwan tied to politics, fuel: Scholars CNA 12 Mar 2026 -- Taiwanese scholars said Thursday that a recent drop in Chinese warplane activity around Taiwan was temporary, likely driven by Beijing's desire for "political security" during the annual "Two Sessions" and higher fuel costs caused by tension in the Middle East.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 12 Mar 2026

  • Sanctions to Disrupt DPRK IT Worker Schemes Defrauding U.S. Businesses US Dept. of State 12 Mar 2026 -- Today, the United States sanctioned two entities and six individuals involved in operations using Information Technology (IT) workers in foreign countries to generate revenue for the Democratic People's Republic of Korea's (DPRK) government.
  • Treasury Sanctions Facilitators of DPRK IT Worker Fraud Targeting U.S. Businesses US Treasury 12 Mar 2026 -- Today, the U.S. Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) sanctioned six individuals and two entities for their roles in Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) government-orchestrated information technology (IT) worker schemes that systematically defraud U.S. businesses and generate revenue to fund the DPRK's weapons of mass destruction (WMD) programs, including nearly $800 million in 2024.

  • Transcript of Weekly Media Briefing by the Official Spokesperson (March 12, 2026) India MEA 12 Mar 2026
  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: NUCLEAR ENERGY PROJECTS India PIB 12 Mar 2026 -- The present installed nuclear power capacity in the country is 8,780 MW comprising of 24 nuclear power plants (excluding RAPS-1 - 100 MW)
  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: DEPLOYMENT OF SMALL MODULAR REACTORS (SMRs) India PIB 12 Mar 2026
  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: RARE EARTH RESERVES IN THE COUNTRY India PIB 12 Mar 2026

  • HRC61 - EU Statement - Interactive dialogue with the Group of Independent Expert on Belarus European External Action Service (EEAS) 12 Mar 2026
  • UN Group of Independent Experts on the Situation of Human Rights in Belarus: the system of impunity is strengthening Latvia MFA 12 Mar 2026 -- On 12 March 2026 in Geneva, a report on the situation of human rights in Belarus will be presented in the UN Human Rights Council.
  • UN Human Rights Council 61: UK Statement for the Group of Experts on Belarus UK FCDO 12 Mar 2026 -- UK Statement for the Interactive Dialogue with the Group of Experts on Belarus. Delivered by the UK's Human Rights Ambassador, Eleanor Sanders.
  • Foreign Minister Baiba Braže meets with the leader of the Belarusian democratic forces, Sviatlana Tsikhanouskaya: Lukashenko's regime continues to support Russia's aggression against Ukraine. Sanctions pressure must continue Latvia MFA 12 Mar 2026 -- On 12 March 2026 the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Baiba Braže, met with the leader of the Belarusian democratic forces and head of the United Transitional Cabinet, Sviatlana Tsikhanouskaya, and discussed the situation in Belarus, including the regime's repression of civil society and Russia's aggression against Ukraine.

  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's telephone conversation with Cuban Foreign Minister Bruno Rodríguez Parrilla Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 12 Mar 2026

  • The Minister of Defence of the Republic of Armenia met with the Minister of Defence and Foreign Trade of the Kingdom of Belgium Ministry of Defence of the Republic of Armenia 12 Mar 2026
  • Today we must protect the Armenia that we have formed. Prime Minister Prime Minister of the Republic of Armenia 12 Mar 2026 -- Today, the regular Cabinet meeting took place today, chaired by the Prime Minister of the Republic of Armenia Nikol Pashinyan.

  • ICC Office of the Prosecutor concludes preliminary examinations into Venezuela II and Lithuania/Belarus ICC 12 Mar 2026 -- The Office of the Prosecutor of the International Criminal Court (ICC) has concluded its preliminary examinations into the Situation in Venezuela II, and the Situation in Lithuania/Belarus, both referrals by States Parties to the Rome Statute.

  • HRC61 - EU Statement - Interactive Dialogue with the Independent International Fact-Finding Mission on the Bolivarian Republic of Venezuela European External Action Service (EEAS) 12 Mar 2026
  • Venezuela: Uncertainty in Venezuela must give way to meaningful human rights change, UN Fact-Finding Mission says OHCHR 12 Mar 2026 -- Venezuela's repressive State machinery, built up over many years, remains operational following the abrupt apprehension of former President Nicolás Maduro by United States forces on 3 January, the UN Independent International Fact-Finding Mission on the Bolivarian Republic of Venezuela said today.
  • Venezuela's repressive State apparatus 'intact' despite leadership change UN News 12 Mar 2026 -- Venezuela's repressive State apparatus remains operational despite the seizure of former President Nicolás Maduro by United States forces on 3 January, according to independent UN human rights investigators.

  • OSCE report: Human rights situation in Georgia has worsened since spring 2024 Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Finland 12 Mar 2026 -- An OSCE Moscow Mechanism report on the developments in Georgia in respect of human rights and fundamental freedoms since spring 2024 was presented at the meeting of the OSCE Permanent Council on 12 March.
  • OSCE Moscow Mechanism Report on Georgia: Joint statement, March 2026 UK FCDO 12 Mar 2026 -- The UK, together with 23 other countries, calls on Georgia to reverse democratic backsliding and to implement in full the recommendations of the 2026 OSCE Moscow Mechanism.

  • Transcript of the Press Briefing by the Spokesperson on Thursday 12th March 2026 Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 12 Mar 2026

  • The Presidents of Lithuania and the Czech Republic visited the Lithuania-Belarus border President of the Republic of Lithuania 12 Mar 2026 -- On Thursday, President Gitanas Nausėda and the President of the Czech Republic, Petr Pavel, currently on a state visit to Lithuania, visited the Lithuania-Belarus border.
  • The Presidents of Lithuania and the Czech Republic discussed strengthening regional security and expanding bilateral cooperation President of the Republic of Lithuania 12 Mar 2026 -- On Thursday, President Gitanas Nausėda met with the President of the Czech Republic, Petr Pavel, who arrived in Lithuania on a state visit.

  • World News in Brief: Deadly drone attacks in Sudan, violence against women in the spotlight, more clashes in DR Congo UN News 12 Mar 2026 -- UN human rights chief Volker Türk said on Thursday he was appalled by the devastating impact on civilians of increasing drone attacks in Sudan, amid reports that more than 200 civilians have been killed by drones since 4 March alone, in the Kordofan region and White Nile state.
